architecture_summary: ViViT is a pure-transformer architecture for video classification that extracts spatio-temporal tokens from video and encodes them with transformer layers. It proposes several efficient variants that factorise spatial and temporal processing, and it leverages pretrained image models plus regularisation to train effectively on smaller video datasets.

novel_contributions: several efficient variants of the model which factorise the spatial- and temporal-dimensions of the input
effective regularisation during training and leveraging pretrained image models to train on comparatively small datasets
thorough ablation studies on tokenisation strategies, model architecture and regularisation methods
state-of-the-art results on multiple video classification benchmarks including Kinetics 400 and 600, Epic Kitchens, Something-Something v2 and Moments in Time

key_claims.claim: ViViT presents pure-transformer based models for video classification that extract spatio-temporal tokens and encode them with transformer layers.
The paper proposes efficient variants that factorise the spatial- and temporal-dimensions of the input video to handle long token sequences.
The model can be effectively regularised and initialised from pretrained image models to train on comparatively small datasets.
ViViT achieves state-of-the-art results on multiple video classification benchmarks including Kinetics 400 and 600, Epic Kitchens, Something-Something v2 and Moments in Time.
The factorised encoder and factorised dot-product variants substantially reduce FLOPs compared with the unfactorised spatio-temporal model.
Using tubelet embedding with central frame initialisation outperforms uniform frame sampling and filter inflation on Kinetics.
Additional regularisation improves Epic Kitchens and SSv2 performance, with a substantial overall improvement of 5.3% on Epic Kitchens.
The best reported results use a ViViT-H backbone pretrained on JFT, reaching 84.9% on Kinetics 400 and 85.8% on Kinetics 600.
